title: Shift Hunch from advisory to merge-blocking enforcement via CI
context: Hunch previously alerted developers to constraint violations; competitive research identified the "killer feature" as automated enforcement. Needed a way to gate PRs without external cloud-RAG dependencies or per-commit provenance loss. Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file).
decision: Introduce a GitHub Action (`hunch-guard.yml`) that runs `hunch check --base` over PR diffs, posts sticky comments citing violated constraints (con_/dec_ ids), and FAILS the check only on direct, high-confidence, non-stale blocking invariants. Constraints live in git history alongside the code; the guard diffs base...head and reads git log to enforce.
consequences: PRs can now be automatically blocked at merge time if they violate a committed constraint
Advisory violations (near/stale/low-confidence) stay as comments, only strict blockers fail the check
Requires explicit setup (`hunch ci` scaffolds the workflow) but is idempotent and self-enforcing
Constraints must be versioned in git (no external dependency, full provenance and auditability)
Works on shared repos because strict gate (v0.11) prevents false positives
alternatives_rejected: Keep advisory-only (v0.11 baseline) — doesn't enforce; no competitive differentiation
Block on all constraint touches — too strict; near/stale issues need human judgment
Use cloud-RAG or note/ADR tools — no per-commit provenance, not reproducible in isolated repos
Pre-commit hook enforcement — misses CI context, can't be required across a shared team
